<!-- generated by sofar — do not hand-edit; truth lives in events.jsonl -->

# Decisions: session-strategy-bench

- 2026-07-19T14:16:42.073Z — chose **Pre-registered falsifiable predictions (P1 cost-climb-vs-flat, P2 quality-parity, P3 arm L wins verbatim-recall-from-early-session — deliberately the opponent's territory, P4 latency-growth) frozen in product doc/session-strategy-bench-evidence.md at initiative creation, before any run** over Post-hoc analysis of whatever the data shows, or predictions written/softened after seeing run 1 because The benchmark must be able to lose — P3 registers a case where the long session is expected to WIN, so a clean sweep for arm S would itself be suspect. Falsified predictions get recorded as falsified; marketing copy derives only from the honest-summary paragraph.
- 2026-07-19T14:16:46.816Z — chose **Measure-only jurisdiction: read-side only, zero changes to engine or injection content; any finding that suggests an engine change is logged as a proposal in this record, never implemented here** over Letting benchmark findings drive engine/injection tweaks in-flight within this initiative because Measuring and optimizing in one initiative contaminates both: the numbers stop being comparable across runs and the changes ship without their own acceptance criteria. Injection byte-stability is a SPEC invariant; this bench depends on the measured surface staying frozen.
- 2026-07-19T14:16:53.221Z — chose **Reuse the handoff-bench machinery: harness/rubric base at ~/IO/handoff-bench, evidence file alongside R04's in product doc/ (sofar-bench format), dual blind graders with >=1 cross-family, "blind LLM-graded, cross-family" label only when both graders ran, and minimum 2 workload types (feature + refactor) before any headline claim** over Building a new bench, single-family (Anthropic-only) grading, or headline claims from one workload because R04's judge-provenance mitigations (cross-family re-grade + founder spot-check, both cleared 2026-07-11) set the credibility bar; a second scenario inherits it by reusing the same machinery instead of re-earning it. Single-workload results are an anecdote, not a claim.
- 2026-07-19T14:44:14.418Z — chose **Arm L runs in a worktree with sofar wiring stripped (sofar uninit --purge; scenario-1 arm-A doctoring precedent); both arms share a frozen TASKS.md committed into the snapshot so task prompts stay byte-identical; arm S's initiative plan is seeded from TASKS.md** over Running arm L with sofar wired (its Stop hook exits 2 on any turn without session_ended, forcing per-task write-backs), or arm-specific prompt wording pointing arm S at the record because The Stop gate makes "session never ends" impossible for a wired arm L and would contaminate the treatment: L must be the pure long-session strategy (context as memory), S the pure sofar pattern (record as memory). TASKS.md as the shared task source is the only way to keep prompts byte-identical while each arm keeps its own memory channel.
- 2026-07-19T15:05:39.039Z — chose **Bench isolation via a dedicated clone (~/IO/brillo-bench @ a6e0c8d2, origin remote removed) with user-named roadmap features as workloads: W1 Meeting Minutes &amp; Summary, W3 AI Chat (optional third); sync-health W1 retired unrun; W2 retargeted at the clone. Feature tasks enforce a no-live-API rule (deterministic reference impls behind provider-agnostic interfaces; acceptance = typecheck + unit tests)** over Running bench worktrees inside the live brillo checkout (prod app), keeping the invented sync-health workload, or letting workload acceptance depend on live model/OAuth calls the bench copy has no credentials for because User direction 2026-07-19: brillo is live prod — duplicate it and bench in the copy. Removing the origin remote makes a push-back to prod impossible by construction; user-named features are real roadmap work (better workload validity than an invented feature); retiring before any run keeps the freeze discipline intact (no prediction touched).
- 2026-07-20T06:10:55.697Z — chose **Publish P1 as SPLIT (arm-S-flat falsified on the feature workload, held on the refactor), P3 as NOT TESTED (probe design failed — both arms imported the constant instead of recalling it), P4 as PARTIALLY SUPPORTED (large erratic latency penalty, not monotonic prefix growth); record the arm-L wall-clock win prominently** over Rewording P1 to "climbs less steeply" as if that had been predicted, quietly dropping the failed probe, reporting only the cost win that favors the product, or treating the workload-type split as noise to be averaged away because The pre-registration exists precisely so a losing prediction gets published as losing; the benchmark had to be able to lose (D1). Arm S won cost on both workloads but lost wall-clock on both — reporting only the former would be marketing, not evidence. Workload-type variance is a finding (scenario-1 precedent), and a probe that fails to discriminate is a methodology result worth more than a fabricated P3 verdict.
